paragraphs.body: My “Cockatiel” Life In “Why Cockatiels Are The Best Pets?”, author Sani Hussain suggests cockatiels are the best pets, not only because they are adorable, but also very intelligent (not all of them are). He argues his point by developing several important ideas. First, Hussain claims cockatiels can mimic humans and do many things that humans can do, such as playing mini basketball. Sometimes they know how to kiss, dance, and speak like humans do. Next, he portrays cockatiels as very affectionate pets who can stay by your side for a long time. When you are busy working on things, they can sit next to you or on your shoulder preening themselves. Even though they want your attention, which can be a little annoying sometimes, they give you unconditional love no matter what’s happening (except when you bully them). Third, Hussain demonstrates that, compared to dogs and cats’ poops, their poops are small and easier to dispose of. He also implies that it’s very simple to take care of them by feeding them on time and cleaning their cage. Finally, the author suggests that not all cockatiels are smart, but if you put a lot of time and effort into them, they can become extremely clever. “Clever” does not describe my cockatiel Color. During the year of COVID-19, my sister and I got so bored that we started begging my mom to buy us a pet to play with. Unfortunately, my mom is a zoophobe who hates animals, especially when they are furry. She believes animals are dirty, scary, and have a high possibility of hurting people, which might lead to having to get a rabies vaccine. She also hates cleaning up poop when she already has four kids to take care of. One of her most famous, or maybe universal, quote is that: “You couldn't feed yourself, let alone the pets.” But her words did not discourage us; instead, we kept begging her day and night, saying that we could use our own money. In the end, we had an agreement–buy a pet that’s not furry and easy to take care of. I wasn't that upset compared to my sister, because she wanted a dog or cat so bad, while I knew my mom was giving up her bottom line.

paragraphs.body: As a result, we bought a cockatiel, because the staff told us they are the best pets to have as a beginner. However, I hate cockatiels because I think they are very unattractive and ugly like a malnourished chick, and especially their nose and head feathers look so weird. They literally look like bananas, and act like a fake, or would-be cockatoo, a larger bird that I adore. Everyone was saying that cockatiels are so cute, but I thought people were just exaggerating because all I saw was that they were eerie and ugly as xxxx (bad word) compared to the majestic cockatoo.

annotations.text: However, I hate cockatiels because I think they are very unattractive and ugly like a malnourished chick, and especially their nose and head feathers look so weird.
annotations.comment: Great details, excellent (hilarious) tone, but the sentence doesn't quite make sense! Try breaking it into multiple clear sentences.

paragraphs.body: Before buying Color, I still remember one thing that’s vivid in my mind that I confidently said to my sister: “The only bird I will never buy is a cockatiel.” However, thinking like this was not helpful anymore because it was the only choice I had. I mean, at least a cockatiel is something. After we brought her home, we started with enthusiasm thinking that we were 100 percent able to take care of her. But as time passed, I noticed that the only thing my bird knew how to do every day was to poop a lot–everywhere. For some reason, she loved to poop on me (head, shoulders, even on my homework)! And eat my homework, too. Unfortunately, you don’t get an excuse from telling your teacher, “My bird pooped or ate my homework.” I still get very mad when she does these things to me, but I can't do anything to stop her. Sometimes my sister slaps her on her back (no worries, it's very soft) to show her anger, but this stupid bird has no idea we are mad. To solve the Pooping Everywhere Problem, I even tried to train her on potty-pooping, but she was too stupid to understand, and so I bought bird diapers. Instead of being appreciative, she looked like Emotional Damage when I put the diaper on her; therefore, I stopped using it. Now I have to suffer from all the poops she makes every half an hour. She also hates showering and keeps flying around the house every time she notices we are going to clean her body. Therefore, my sister and I must chase her around the house and climb up tall cabinets to try to catch and lock her in the bathroom so she can take her shower. However, she goes crazy when she touches the water and flies towards me, leaving scratches on my face.

annotations.text: Instead of being appreciative, she looked like Emotional Damage

paragraphs.body: As time passed, we gradually accepted the fact that she was what she was, even though she kept making the mess. Cleaning her cage and spending time with her had become my daily routine. She became so affectionate and wanted my attention through our interaction. As she joined my family, I realized that I had changed my opinion on cockatiels and that I was willing to take care of this bird despite its problems. Without a doubt, love can be about service, about giving more so than about receiving. Sometimes when I sit down with Color on my thigh, I flashback to when my mom described me when I was little. Did I also poop a lot? Was I so ignorant that maybe she also wanted to slap me on my back? I know she said I hated eating when I was a kid, so three people had to chase after me, trying to get me to eat: my 干妈, 婆婆, and 爷 爷. From kitchen to bedroom, from bedroom to the yard.

annotations.text: As she joined my family, I realized that I had changed my opinion on cockatiels and that I was willing to take care of this bird despite its problems. Without a doubt, love can be about service, about giving more so than about receiving.
annotations.comment: Oh this is lovely! Great move!! Is this idea at the heart of this essay? Is this the central focusing idea that has value and purpose beyond yourself?

paragraphs.body: In addition to chasing me in order to get me to eat, I would steal my mom’s high heels, argue over a toy, or just cry a lot. They must have been so cooked with me because I did it every day. One thing I remember vividly is one time when I was little. My 干妈 bought her daughter 贝贝 (who I called my cousin, even though she is not) a mini candy machine toy. I saw it and wanted it because it’s cute, and who can resist an adorable toy? But my cousin also wanted her toy, so I started crying and said I want that toy. 干妈 saw me crying and told 贝贝 to give the toy to me. Then 贝贝 started to cry and said that toy was hers and she didn’t want to give it to me. But 干妈 didn’t listen to her and gave her toy to me. I have felt very bad about this since elementary school, although 贝贝 said she can’t remember that happening. Even though I wasn't counted as their real blood-related child, they treated me like I was their true kid, treated me like I was their little angel.

paragraphs.body: When I went back to my biological family, I still continued making troubles. My mom said I cried a lot when I didn't get the toy I wanted or didn’t want to come back from the playground. Because we had so many kids, my mom hired three babysitters to work in our house. When I was little, I didn't understand human relationships and was very straight forward and stupid. When I hear one of them saying bad things about another babysitter, I snitched to them what I heard: “Hey, I heard XXX said you are such XXX.” And yes, same for all three. As a result, they got into a fight with each other and ended up leaving our house. After that, my mom was not able to find other babysitters willing to help, and she started to take care of four children on her own. Even though I’m the reason this whole awful thing happened, my mom never blamed me for it. And yes, she was always moving her bottom line to allow me to do the things I wanted, like buying my cockatiel. Remember, even though she hates animals, she was still willing to buy me a bird.

All of them - my mom 妈妈, my 干妈，婆婆，爷爷，贝贝 - they are all my true family. They never complain (but sometimes joke about how I was such a bad kid). They were exhausted but stayed by my side, and put me into my sweet dream. In the essay, “Why Cockatiels Are The Best Pets?”, Hussian writes, “Even when you go, as soon as you come back they will be waiting for you. Some cockatiels can also be free roam [sic], and will stay by your side for long periods of time”(Hussain 1). In other words, Hussian is saying cockatiels will always be there for you, just like my family are always here to support me, no matter what I did or do wrong. Like cockatiels, they give me unconditional love. On the other hand, I’m just like a little cockatiel to them, annoying and troublesome, but there is an invisible bond between us. They love me, no matter what I did or do, just as I got obsessed with my infuriating cockatiel.

annotations.text: just as I got obsessed with my infuriating cockatie
annotations.context_left: r what I did or do,

paragraphs.body: Many people argue that the blood is more important than all the effort and emotion you put into a person outside of your family. I disagree because humans are like cockatiels. Just as Hussain argues, “Not least cockatiels are intelligent birds. If owners train them from the beginning or put effort in them they can be very loving”(Hussain 1). Hussain believes the time you spend on something is way more important, and it acts like a magical potion that changes things from hate to love. I gave so much of my time and attention to Color that now I think cockatiels are cute and adorable. Their nose and head feathers are no longer weird to me. And the fact that they look like bananas also somehow changed; they do look like bananas though, but a cute banana that is so teeny-tiny adorable that it makes me wanna cry and kiss them so hard. And their smells too, so milky that I wanna eat them in one bite. For those who like babies, the babies are cute maybe because they are stupid, and to me, my bird is cute maybe because she is also very stupid. But I love her, just like my family loves me even when I’m brainless. Even though they are not here next to me, our love and feelings are nevertheless passing through time and space, and bonded strongly between us.

annotations.text: gave so much of my time and attention to Color that now I think cockatiels are cute and adorable. Their nose and head feathers are no longer weird to me. And the fact that they look like bananas also somehow changed; they do look like bananas though, but a cute banana that is so teeny-tiny adorable that it makes me wanna cry and kiss them so hard.
